response.reasoning: Bogdan has over 12 years of full-stack experience, far exceeding the 6+ year requirement, with consistent work in TypeScript, Node.js, React, and cloud platforms (AWS CDK, Docker, Kubernetes). He has direct experience with AI-agent infrastructure and MCP code generators at re:cinq, aligning with the required AI agent development skill. His work spans frontend (React, Ember.js), backend (Node.js, DLang, Java), databases (MongoDB, PostgreSQL/MySQL implied), and testing (TDD, CI/CD, Vitest-like frameworks). He has contributed to architecture, scalability, and developer tooling, and holds a CS bachelor’s degree. His open-source projects and workshops demonstrate strong communication, collaboration, and process improvement — key for internal tooling. Minor gaps: no explicit mention of Nest.js or Vitest, but his Node.js/TypeScript/testing experience is deep enough to imply adaptability. German language is basic, but role is in Berlin with English as primary work language.
strengths: 12+ years of full-stack experience with strong TypeScript/Node.js/React proficiency and AI-agent development exposure
Proven track record in architecting scalable systems, cloud deployment (AWS, Docker, Kubernetes), and DevOps practices
Active contributor to open-source tools and developer education, demonstrating communication, collaboration, and process improvement
concerns: No explicit mention of Nest.js or Vitest in current stack — though equivalent experience exists
German language proficiency is only basic, which may limit informal team integration in Berlin
Extensive history with DLang and Java may suggest preference for non-standard tools, potentially slowing onboarding to primary stack
response.justification: Bogdan exceeds all core requirements with deep full-stack expertise, proven AI-agent work, and strong architectural and process skills. His open-source contributions and teaching background indicate exceptional collaboration and communication. While he lacks explicit Nest.js/Vitest experience, his mastery of equivalent technologies (Node.js, TypeScript, testing frameworks) makes adaptation highly likely. The minor language and tooling gaps are outweighed by his proven impact and adaptability.
